sample.lesson: A wedge with an inclination of angle θ rests next to a wall. A box of mass m is sliding down the incline surface, as shown. There is no friction between any of the surfaces (wedge and the box, wedge and the horizontal surface, wedge and the wall). For the answers below, you can use θ, m and g as needed. (a) Find Ftotal, the magnitude of the sum of all forces acting on the box. (b) Find Fww, the magnitude of the force exerted by the wall on the wedge.

sample.lesson: A sled of mass m is released from rest at the top of a snowy hill that makes an angle θ with the horizontal. The sled slides down the incline, as shown. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the sled and the hill is μ_k. Take g = 9.8 m/s^2. Let x be the distance measured along the incline from the release point. (a) Find Fnet, the magnitude of the net force acting on the sled along the incline. (b) Find v_x, the speed of the sled when it reaches position x on the incline.

sample.lesson: A wedge with an inclination of angle θ rests next to a wall. Two boxes are on the incline surface and are connected by a light string, as shown. The lower box has mass m2 and is sliding down the incline surface, pulling the upper box of mass m1. There is no friction between any of the surfaces, and the string is massless. Take g = 9.8 m/s^2.
